name: Brooklyn Heights Clay Parapet, Left Flank
description: A high earthwork rampart of clay and timber, its fire grates blazing with green pine to simulate an occupied garrison. Behind the parapet: silence. The fortifications that held nine thousand men an hour ago are empty in every direction.
atmosphericDetails: The snap and hiss of green pine burning in the fire grates, throwing jumping shadows across the empty earthworks behind the parapet.
The click of British equipment belts and the crunch of boots on gravel, audible when the wind shifts—less than two hundred yards south.
The hollow echo of entrenching spades struck against hard earth by Mifflin's men, simulating ongoing construction work.
The smell of sulfur from signal rockets fired earlier in the evening, still faint in the wet air.
